summary: Frontend canonical alignment testing complete. All 12 test cases PASSED. Verified: (1) is_demo blocks removed from AgentLayout.js and BuyerLayout.js - no DEMO badges visible, (2) billing field names aligned to Phase 4 backend contract (unit_usage, can_create_unit, property_limit), (3) usage_percent and near_limit computed locally in frontend, (4) demo agent and buyer login flows work end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: AGENTLAYOUT.JS VERIFIED: No is_demo block - DEMO badge removed
BUYERLAYOUT.JS VERIFIED: No is_demo block - DEMO badge removed
AGENTBILLING.JS VERIFIED: Uses unit_usage (not property_usage), can_create_unit (not can_create_property), usage_percent computed locally
AGENTPROJECTS.JS VERIFIED: Uses unit_usage, can_create_unit, near_limit computed locally (>= 80% of property_limit)
AGENTSETTINGS.JS VERIFIED: Uses unit_usage, usage_percent computed locally
BACKEND CONTRACT VERIFIED: /api/billing/status returns unit_usage, can_create_unit, property_limit (not property_usage, can_create_property)
seed_data_creation: Demo seed via POST /api/demo/seed creates agent with 'pro' plan
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend canonical alignment complete. All billing pages now use unit_usage (not property_usage) and can_create_unit (not can_create_property). The is_demo UI blocks have been removed from both AgentLayout.js and BuyerLayout.js. Backend billing/status endpoint returns: plan_id, plan_name, property_limit, current_unit_count, subscription_status, subscription_period_end, stripe_subscription_id, stripe_customer_id, unit_limit, unit_usage, can_create_unit.
